event: Autel Robotics (CN-manufactured, 90% adversary components) faces coordinated state AG challenges Q3-Q4 2026 using TX AG passthrough template — FCC equipment freeze + NDAA §848 enforcement create legal squeeze play forcing US entity recertification or exit
evidence_basis: Autel flagged as 90% adversary component sourcing + China manufacturing. FCC equipment authorization freeze (Dec 2025) blocks new imports. TX AG template established precedent for state-level NDAA enforcement. Autel's hybrid CN/US structure makes it vulnerable to coordinated federal-state action unlike pure-CN DJI or pure-rebadge Anzu.
probability_basis: Strong inference from regulatory convergence: FCC freeze operational, TX AG template proven, Autel's ambiguous entity status creates legal attack surface. No primary source confirms timing, but regulatory tools are active.
drivers: FCC equipment authorization freeze blocks new Autel model imports
NDAA §848 enforcement escalates with state AG coordination
Autel's CN manufacturing + US presence creates jurisdictional vulnerability

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: FCC import freeze + state NDAA enforcement converge on hybrid CN/US entities — Autel most exposed

event: DJI Ninth Circuit case 26-1029 likely remanded or narrowly upheld Q4 2026 — does NOT overturn FCC Covered List authority but may impose procedural safeguards on future entity additions, leaving Jan 2027 firmware cliff intact
evidence_basis: FCC Covered List firmware prohibition (Jan 1 2027) creates operational cliff per existing flag. No intel articles mention DJI litigation status, but regulatory timeline suggests case resolution needed before firmware deadline. Courts historically defer to FCC technical authority but impose procedural requirements.
probability_basis: Credible signal from timing: firmware cliff Jan 2027 implies judicial resolution Q4 2026. Probability reflects judicial unpredictability and lack of case status intel. Historical pattern: agencies win on substance, lose on procedure.
drivers: FCC Covered List firmware prohibition Jan 1 2027 deadline pressures judicial calendar
Courts typically defer to FCC on national security technical determinations
Procedural safeguards politically viable compromise preserving FCC authority

event: NATO Admiral warns Russian imperial ambitions drive threat assessment — validates Blue UAS program expansion + NATO interoperability requirements for Skydio/Parrot/BRINC platforms in Eastern European allied procurements H2 2026
evidence_basis: United24 Apr 27: Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone (NATO Military Committee) confirms Russia remains 'number one threat, driven by imperial ambitions to reclaim former USSR states.' Ohio DFR pilot pre-approved vendor list (Skydio, BRINC, Parrot) signals Blue UAS standardization. Drone Dominance EO drives DCMA Blue UAS list transition.
probability_basis: Strong inference: NATO threat assessment + US Blue UAS vendor consolidation (Ohio pilot example) + Drone Dominance EO create aligned procurement incentives. No primary source confirms NATO Blue UAS adoption timeline, but pattern is clear.
drivers: NATO top military official confirms Russian expansionist threat Apr 27 2026
Ohio DFR pilot establishes Blue UAS vendor precedent (Skydio/BRINC/Parrot)
Drone Dominance EO transitions Blue UAS list to DCMA — NATO interoperability requirement likely

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: NATO threat posture + Blue UAS standardization drive allied procurement alignment

event: Validation: Gray zone entity STABLE scoring (all deltas +0.0000) understates regulatory velocity — FCC firmware cliff Jan 2027 + equipment freeze create compounding pressure not reflected in static risk scores. Anzu/SkyRover/SPECTA face functional ban regardless of score.
evidence_basis: Gray zone score deltas show: SkyRover, Anzu, SPECTA, Cogito all +0.0000 STABLE. But regulatory environment changed: FCC equipment freeze (Dec 2025) operational, firmware prohibition Jan 1 2027 imminent per existing flag. Static scores miss regulatory cliff effect — entities can't import new hardware OR update existing firmware.
probability_basis: Near-certain from primary regulatory sources: FCC freeze confirmed, firmware deadline verified. STABLE scores are mathematically accurate but strategically misleading — they measure entity behavior, not regulatory environment change.
drivers: FCC equipment authorization freeze (Dec 2025) blocks new gray zone imports
FCC Covered List firmware prohibition (Jan 1 2027) creates update ban
Static risk scoring methodology doesn't capture regulatory environment velocity

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Regulatory environment change creates functional ban independent of entity risk score — methodology gap

event: Ukraine-Norway joint mid-strike drone production (several thousand units announced Apr 27) validates NATO Blue UAS demand + creates Skydio/Parrot production pressure — Norwegian financing model likely template for allied co-production Q3-Q4 2026
evidence_basis: United24 Apr 27: Ukraine Ministry of Defense announces 'several thousand mid-strike drones' produced in Norway, Norwegian-financed, first joint UAV production initiative. Ohio DFR pilot shows Blue UAS vendor consolidation. Drone Dominance EO $1.4B appropriation drives allied interoperability.
probability_basis: Credible signal: Ukraine-Norway deal confirmed, 'several thousand' is vague but meaningful scale. Probability reflects uncertainty on whether Norway model replicates to other allies (Poland, Baltics likely) and timeline ambiguity. Blue UAS vendors positioned but capacity unknown.
drivers: Ukraine-Norway co-production announced Apr 27 with Norwegian financing
NATO threat assessment (Russian imperialism) drives allied procurement coordination
Blue UAS vendor list (Skydio, Parrot, BRINC) established via Ohio DFR pilot precedent

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Allied co-production financing model + Blue UAS standardization create NATO supply chain pressure
